title: SK Hynix seeks to raise $28 billion in its US IPO
summary: The memory chip maker plans to list on Wall Street, attracting interest from hedge funds and retail investors.

title: Wall Street closes higher driven by semiconductor rebound
summary: The Nasdaq rises more than 1% led by chip stocks, which recover after two weeks of declines.

title: EasyJet agrees to sale to Castlelake for £5.5 billion
summary: The low-cost airline soars on stock market after reaching acquisition agreement with the investment fund.

summary: Investors and funds position themselves in the memory sector, with SK Hynix and Samsung warning Micron about a possible oversupply.

title: Wave of acquisitions: Criteo, Singular Bank, and CVC Marina
summary: Tech company Criteo receives a buyout offer, ING acquires Spanish bank Singular Bank, and CVC sells its marina business for more than one billion.

title: Artificial intelligence transforms banking and regulation, while Amazon enters AI chips market
summary: AI revolutionizes the financial sector, regulators warn about risks, and Amazon challenges Nvidia in the AI chip market.

title: Bond markets: rates stable in Europe, Fed doubts about guidance, and effect of Iran crisis on QT
summary: German bond yields remain flat, the Fed debates rate communication, and the Iran crisis affects the QT program.

summary: Investors compare short-term bond ETFs, Euribor falls below 2.7%, and Germany debates its pension reform.

title: Ibex falls below 19,700 points after correction from highs
summary: The Spanish index extends its decline ahead of corporate earnings.

title: OPEC+ agrees to increase production and oil falls as China buys more crude
summary: OPEC+ increases supply by 188,000 barrels per day, pressuring prices, and China takes advantage to increase purchases.

title: Gold remains depressed and Citi joins London clearing market
summary: The precious metal continues to decline and Citigroup prepares to integrate the London gold clearing system.

title: Big investors allocate billions to private credit despite volatility
summary: Institutional investors continue to bet on private credit in search of returns amid market uncertainty.

title: Trump rings Wall Street bell from Oval Office and Dow Jones reaches new milestone
summary: The former president symbolically participates in the market opening, celebrating a new Dow Jones record.

title: First Solar and Solaria lead gains in solar sector
summary: First Solar soars on sector news, while Solaria surpasses key technical levels on the Spanish stock exchange.

title: Tesla, Joby Aviation, and Bezos' electric truck: electric mobility updates
summary: Tesla presents good news for investors, Joby Aviation soars on stock market, and Bezos launches the cheapest electric truck in the US with Chinese batteries.

title: Alphabet, Broadcom, and a buying opportunity in chips
summary: Alphabet shows buying potential after falling 10%, Broadcom extends its agreement with Apple until 2031, and an analyst sees possible 625% rise in a semiconductor stock.

title: China launches missiles from submarines and NATO shows concern
summary: Chinese armed forces carry out missile launches, including one from a submarine, generating alarm in NATO and criticism from Taiwan.

summary: Nasdaq.com publishes analysis on AT&T ahead of earnings, comparison between Altria and Philip Morris, doubts about Costco, and an ETF that beats the S&P 500.

title: Michael Saylor sells €216 million in Bitcoin

title: Japan: pension funds perform at best and MUFG aims to be global top 5
